introSection: Formule 1 was a steel wild mouse roller coaster previously located at Parc Saint Paul in Saint-Paul, Hauts-de-France, France. It was built by Pax Company.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened on 12 June 2005.

Seatbelts were added in 2009 after a fatal accident on the same ride.

The seatbelts were removed in 2020. Park management reported that they were "not functional".

The ride permanently closed after a fatal accident on 4 July 2020.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: This wild mouse roller coaster was unusual in that the tight turns and switchbacks bank outwards.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content:
On 4 July 2020, a 32-year-old woman was killed after falling from a moving car.

In October 2025, the park's former owner appeared in court over the 2020 incident. The prosecution alleged that removing the seatbelts contributed to the accident, whereas the defense claimed the former owner was being used as a scapegoat and that previous investigations concluded that the ride met safety standards even without the belts.

fullText:
Formule 1 was a steel wild mouse roller coaster previously located at Parc Saint Paul in Saint-Paul, Hauts-de-France, France. It was built by Pax Company.

The ride opened on 12 June 2005.

Seatbelts were added in 2009 after a fatal accident on the same ride.

The seatbelts were removed in 2020. Park management reported that they were "not functional".

The ride permanently closed after a fatal accident on 4 July 2020.

This wild mouse roller coaster was unusual in that the tight turns and switchbacks bank outwards.

Blue track and white supports.

Grand Prix car theme.

On 4 July 2020, a 32-year-old woman was killed after falling from a moving car.

In October 2025, the park's former owner appeared in court over the 2020 incident. The prosecution alleged that removing the seatbelts contributed to the accident, whereas the defense claimed the former owner was being used as a scapegoat and that previous investigations concluded that the ride met safety standards even without the belts.
